subject: pwnlib.util.iters.lookahead
truth: Stop using pwnlib.util.iters.lookahead; it has been removed entirely.
quote: remove pwnlib.util.iters.lookahead (broken anyway)

subject: Python version requirement
truth: Stop using Python 2.7 or Python <3.10; pwntools now requires Python 3.10 or newer.
quote: Drop Python 2.7 support / Require Python 3.10

subject: packing utilities `sign` parameter
old: sign parameter accepted as positional argument
truth: Stop passing `sign` as a positional argument to packing utilities; it now requires keyword syntax.
quote: Properly make use of extra arguments in `packing` utilities. `sign` parameter requires keyword syntax to specify it.

from_fact: sign parameter accepted as positional argument

subject: process.recv on Windows when stdout closes
old: process.recv did not raise on stdout close on Windows
new: process.recv raises EOFError when stdout closes on Windows
truth: Stop expecting process.recv to silently handle stdout close on Windows; it now raises EOFError.
quote: Raise EOFError during `process.recv` when stdout closes on Windows

from_fact: process.recv did not raise on stdout close on windows
process.recv raises eoferror when stdout closes on windows

subject: pwn asm -e flag
truth: Stop using the `-e` flag in `pwn asm`; it is deprecated.
quote: Refactor zsh completion script and deprecate `-e` in `pwn asm`

subject: tube.recvline* keepends argument
truth: Stop using 'keepends' argument in tube.recvline* methods; use 'drop' argument instead.
quote: Deprecate 'keepends' argument in favor of 'drop' in `tube.recvline*`

subject: direct commandline scripts invocation
old: direct commandline scripts invocation (e.g. `pwn asm` as a script)
new: use the pwn command with subcommands
truth: Stop invoking pwntools scripts directly from the command line; use the 'pwn' command with subcommands instead.
quote: Deprecate direct commandline scripts invocation and exclude nonsense ones

from_fact: direct commandline scripts invocation (e.g. `pwn asm` as a script)
use the pwn command with subcommands

subject: context.newline
old: context.newline default value when context.os is set to "windows"
new: context.newline changes when setting context.os to "windows"
truth: Stop relying on context.newline staying unchanged when setting context.os to "windows"; it now changes automatically.
quote: Change `context.newline` when setting `context.os` to `"windows"`

from_fact: context.newline default value when context.os is set to "windows"
context.newline changes when setting context.os to "windows"

subject: tube.readline()
new: tube.readline() returns buffered data on first EOF
truth: Stop assuming tube.readline() returns empty or raises on first EOF; it now returns any buffered data before the EOF.
quote: Return buffered data on first EOF in tube.readline()

from_fact: tube.readline() returns buffered data on first eof

subject: apport_corefile() output type
truth: Stop treating apport_corefile() output as bytes-like; it now returns a string.
quote: Convert apport_corefile() output from bytes-like object to string

subject: process environment handling
old: process() replaces the entire environment when env= is passed
new: process() now allows adding to the existing environment instead of replacing it
truth: process() env parameter now merges with the existing environment by default instead of replacing it entirely
quote: Allow to add to the existing environment in `process` instead of replacing it

from_fact: process() replaces the entire environment when env= is passed
process() now allows adding to the existing environment instead of replacing it

subject: gdb.debug exe parameter
old: gdb.debug did not respect the exe parameter and required a non-empty argv
new: gdb.debug now respects the exe parameter and allows empty argv
truth: gdb.debug now respects the exe parameter and allows an empty argv list
quote: Fix gdb.debug: exe parameter now respected, allow empty argv

from_fact: gdb.debug did not respect the exe parameter and required a non-empty argv
gdb.debug now respects the exe parameter and allows empty argv

subject: gdb.debug shell execution
old: gdb.debug went through a shell to launch the debugged process
new: gdb.debug no longer goes through a shell
truth: gdb.debug no longer launches the debugged process through a shell
quote: Don't go though a shell in `gdb.debug`

from_fact: gdb.debug went through a shell to launch the debugged process
gdb.debug no longer goes through a shell

subject: shellcraft dup()
truth: Stop using shellcraft dup(); use dupio() instead — it has been renamed consistently across all supported architectures.
quote: shellcraft: dup() is now called dupio() consistently across all supported arches

subject: asm() headers for x86-32, x86-64, mips
new: asm() no longer emits p2align 2 headers
truth: asm() no longer emits p2align 2 headers for x86-32, x86-64, and mips architectures, which changes instruction length when patching binaries.
quote: Removes `p2align 2` `asm()` headers from `x86-32`, `x86-64` and `mips` architectures to avoid inconsistent instruction length when patching binaries

from_fact: asm() no longer emits p2align 2 headers

subject: Buffer's parent class
old: Buffer inherited from some other class
truth: Buffer's parent class changed to object, which may break code relying on Buffer's previous inheritance hierarchy.
quote: Change Buffer's parent class to object

from_fact: buffer inherited from some other class

subject: PwnlibException
truth: PwnlibException no longer has sys.exc_type; use self.message and sys.exc_info() instead.
quote: add `self.message` and change `sys.exc_type` to `sys.exec_info()` in PwnlibException

subject: pwnlib imports
truth: pwnlib no longer does unconditional imports; several modules are now deferred to save startup time, which may break code relying on side effects of those imports.
quote: Defer import of several modules to save on startup time

subject: pwnlib.adb
truth: Stop using pwnlib.adb; Android tests are disabled and it is no longer supported in CI.
quote: Disable all Android tests, `pwnlib.adb` is no longer supported in CI

subject: eval CLI command
truth: Stop using the eval CLI command; it has been removed.
quote: Remove eval from cli

subject: pwn.toplevel.__all__
truth: Stop relying on pwn.toplevel.__all__; it has been removed.
quote: Remove unnecessary `pwn.toplevel.__all__`

subject: run_in_new_terminal with gdb
old: gdb not terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal
new: gdb is terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal
truth: run_in_new_terminal now terminates gdb by default; existing code relying on gdb staying open will break.
quote: Misc `run_in_new_terminal` improvements (notably gdb terminated by default)

from_fact: gdb not terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal
gdb is terminated by default when using run_in_new_terminal

subject: process executable lookup
old: process() only looked at system PATH for the executable
new: process() also looks at env['PATH'] to find the executable
truth: process() now also looks at env['PATH'] to find the executable, which may change which binary is selected if env['PATH'] differs from system PATH.
quote: process also looks now at `env['PATH']` to find the path for the executable

from_fact: process() only looked at system path for the executable
process() also looks at env['path'] to find the executable

subject: tubes default newline
truth: Tubes now use `context.newline` instead of a hardcoded newline character by default.
quote: Use `context.newline` for tubes by default

subject: Docker images Python version
old: Docker images used Python2 by default
new: Docker images use Python3 by default
truth: Docker images (`pwntools/pwntools:stable` etc) now use Python3 by default instead of Python2.
quote: Docker images (`pwntools/pwntools:stable` etc) now use Python3 by default

from_fact: docker images used python2 by default
docker images use python3 by default

subject: python2 shebangs
truth: Python2 shebangs have been removed from scripts where appropriate.
quote: Remove python2 shebangs where appropriate

subject: tubes.ssh.process()
old: tubes.ssh.process() required Python 2 installed on remote
new: tubes.ssh.process() no longer requires Python 2 on remote (still requires Python)
truth: Stop relying on Python 2 being installed on the remote host when using tubes.ssh.process(); it now only requires Python (any version).
quote: tubes.ssh.process() no longer requires python 2 installed on remote (still requires python, though)

from_fact: tubes.ssh.process() required python 2 installed on remote
tubes.ssh.process() no longer requires python 2 on remote (still requires python)

subject: packing.flat() filler parameter
old: flat(..., filler=) with str values or Python2 bytes may have worked differently
new: flat(..., filler=) is fixed for str values and Python2 bytes
truth: Stop passing str or Python2 bytes as filler to flat() expecting the old (broken) behaviour; the fix may change how filler values are handled.
quote: flat(..., filler=) is fixed for `str` values and Python2 `bytes`

from_fact: flat(..., filler=) with str values or python2 bytes may have worked differently
flat(..., filler=) is fixed for str values and python2 bytes

subject: packing.flat() field ordering
truth: Stop relying on flat() producing mis-ordered fields; the fix corrects field ordering which may change output.
quote: Fix issues in `packing.flat` with mis-ordred fields

subject: six dependency version requirement
new: pwntools requires six v1.12.0 or higher
truth: Stop using six versions below 1.12.0 with pwntools; you must upgrade six to v1.12.0 or higher.
quote: Pwntools requires `six` v.1.12.0 or higher

from_fact: pwntools requires six v1.12.0 or higher

subject: flat() and fit()
old: flat() and fit() were separate functions
new: flat() and fit() are now the same function
truth: flat() and fit() are now the same function; code relying on them being distinct may silently change behaviour.
quote: `flat()` and `fit()` are now the same function

from_fact: flat() and fit() were separate functions
flat() and fit() are now the same function

subject: IntervalTree version requirement
truth: IntervalTree 3.xx is disallowed; only IntervalTree 2.xx is supported.
quote: Use IntervalTree 2.xx, disallow use of 3.xx

subject: logging color configuration mechanism
old: old undocumented mechanism for changing logging colors
truth: Stop using the old undocumented mechanism for changing logging colors; use the pwnlib.config module with settings in ~/.pwn.conf instead.
quote: This replaces the old, **undocumented** mechanism for changing logging colors.

from_fact: old undocumented mechanism for changing logging colors

subject: process constants
truth: Stop using process.STDOUT, process.PIPE, process.PTY; use the global STDOUT, PIPE, PTY constants instead.
quote:
Move `STDOUT`, `PIPE`, `PTY` constants to globals
    + `process(..., stdin=process.PTY)` --> `process(..., stdin=PTY)`

subject: packing.fit()
old: packing.fit() treated large offsets as raw integers
new: packing.fit() now treats large offsets as cyclic patterns
truth: packing.fit() now treats large offsets (e.g. 0x61616161) as cyclic patterns (same as 'aaaa') instead of raw integers.
quote: `packing.fit()` now treats large offsets as cyclic patterns (e.g. `0x61616161` behaves the same as `"aaaa"`)

from_fact: packing.fit() treated large offsets as raw integers
packing.fit() now treats large offsets as cyclic patterns

subject: sh_string return value
old: sh_string returned empty string for empty input
truth: sh_string now returns a quoted empty string '' instead of an empty string for empty input.
quote: `sh_string` now returns a quoted empty string `''` rather than just an empty string

from_fact: sh_string returned empty string for empty input

subject: ROP class context.bytes
old: ROP class used hard-coded value of 4 for bytes
truth: ROP class now respects context.bytes instead of using the hard-coded value of 4.
quote: The `ROP` class now respects `context.bytes` instead of using the hard-coded value of `4`

from_fact: rop class used hard-coded value of 4 for bytes

subject: ssh.process() return type
old: ssh.process() returned a generic process object
truth: ssh.process() now returns a specialized ssh_process class instead of a generic process object.
quote: Changed the way that `ssh.process()` works internally, and it now returns a more specialized class, `ssh_process`.

from_fact: ssh.process() returned a generic process object

subject: sh_string
new: sh_string emits strings that are no longer copy-paste compatible
truth: Stop relying on sh_string output being copy-paste compatible; the new output embeds single-quoted non-printable literals and works only with tubes and subprocess modules.
quote: Emitted strings are no longer copy-paste compatible, but work fine with e.g. `tubes` module and the default `subprocess` module

from_fact: sh_string emits strings that are no longer copy-paste compatible

subject: shellcraft.sh
old: shellcraft.sh did not provide argv[0] or set argc==1
new: shellcraft.sh now provides argv[0] and sets argc==1 on all platforms
truth: shellcraft.sh now always provides argv[0] and sets argc==1, which may break systems that relied on the old behaviour (e.g. minimal shells that previously worked with argc==0).
quote: Modified `shellcraft.sh` on all platforms to provide `argv[0]` and set `argc==1`

from_fact: shellcraft.sh did not provide argv[0] or set argc==1
shellcraft.sh now provides argv[0] and sets argc==1 on all platforms

subject: process stdout/stderr
new: stdout and stderr are now PTYs by default
truth: Stop assuming process stdout and stderr are pipes; they are now PTYs by default, which changes buffering and read behaviour.
quote: `stdout` and `stderr` are now PTYs by default

from_fact: stdout and stderr are now ptys by default

subject: ssh.run() method
truth: Stop using ssh.run() for running commands over SSH; use ssh.process() instead.
quote: `ssh` objects now have a `ssh.process()` method which avoids the need to handle shell expansion via the old `ssh.run()` method

subject: listen_remote() method
truth: Stop using listen_remote(); use listen() instead.
quote: Added `listen()` method alias for `listen_remote()`

subject: connect_remote() method
truth: Stop using connect_remote(); use remote() instead.
quote: Added `remote()` method alias for `connect_remote()`

subject: bundled binutils
truth: Stop relying on bundled binutils; you must build them yourself as documented.
quote: Removed bundled binutils in favor of documenting how to build them yourselves
